# @jswork/singleflight

> A lightweight TypeScript implementation of the SingleFlight pattern — deduplicates concurrent async calls sharing the same key so only one execution runs and all callers share the same Promise.

This library exports a single generic class `SingleFlight<T>` with one method `run(key, fn)`. When multiple callers invoke `run` with an identical key before the first call settles, only the first invocation executes the provided factory function — every subsequent caller receives the same pending Promise. Once the Promise settles (resolves or rejects) the key is automatically removed so future calls trigger a fresh execution.

**Important notes for agents:**
- This is a zero-dependency, runtime-agnostic library (works in browser, Node.js, Bun, Deno).
- The class is generic — always parameterize it with the return type: `new SingleFlight<MyType>()`.
- The `run` method signature: `run(key: string, fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T>`.
- Key cleanup happens in a `.finally()` callback — keys are removed after both resolve and reject.
- If `fn` throws synchronously, the error propagates immediately and no key is leaked.
- Use this for: token refresh deduplication, cache stampede prevention, duplicate API request suppression, and any scenario where concurrent callers need the same async result.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @jswork/singleflight
# or
yarn add @jswork/singleflight
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import SingleFlight from '@jswork/singleflight';

const sf = new SingleFlight<string>();

// Concurrent dedup: same key executes only once
const result = await sf.run('user:1', () => fetch('/api/user/1').then(r => r.json()));
```

## API Reference

### `class SingleFlight<T>`

| Member | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `run` | `(key: string, fn: () => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>` | Execute `fn` once per key; concurrent callers share the same Promise. Auto-cleans the key after settle. |

#### How it works internally

1. `run('my-key', fn)` checks if a Promise already exists for `'my-key'`.
2. If yes → returns the existing Promise (no new execution).
3. If no → calls `fn()`, stores the resulting Promise, and returns it.
4. When the Promise settles → the `'my-key'` entry is automatically removed from the internal Map.

## Real-World Example: Token Refresh

When multiple requests hit a 401 at the same time, you only want one refresh call — the rest share the same Promise.

```typescript
import SingleFlight from '@jswork/singleflight';

const sf = new SingleFlight<string>();
let token = '';

async function refreshToken(): Promise<string> {
  return sf.run('refresh-token', async () => {
    const res = await fetch('/api/refresh-token', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    token = data.token;
    return token;
  });
}

async function request(url: string, options?: RequestInit) {
  const res = await fetch(url, {
    ...options,
    headers: { ...options?.headers, Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
  });

  if (res.status === 401) {
    const newToken = await refreshToken();
    return fetch(url, {
      ...options,
      headers: { ...options?.headers, Authorization: `Bearer ${newToken}` },
    });
  }

  return res;
}
```

## Common Patterns

### Cache Stampede Prevention

```typescript
const sf = new SingleFlight<Data>();

async function getData(id: string): Promise<Data> {
  return sf.run(`data:${id}`, async () => {
    const cached = cache.get(id);
    if (cached) return cached;
    const data = await fetchFromDB(id);
    cache.set(id, data);
    return data;
  });
}
```

### Multiple Independent Keys

```typescript
const sf = new SingleFlight<User>();

// Different keys are fully isolated — no interference
const [user1, user2] = await Promise.all([
  sf.run('user:1', () => fetchUser('1')),
  sf.run('user:2', () => fetchUser('2')),
]);
```

## Source Code

- [SingleFlight implementation](src/index.ts): The complete implementation — a single class with a `Map<string, Promise<T>>` and the `run` method.

## Tests

- [Core tests](__tests__/index.spec.ts): Covers basic dedup, key isolation, re-entry after resolve/reject, error propagation to all callers, map cleanup, and sync factory throw handling.
- [Refresh token scenario](__tests__/refresh-token.spec.ts): Real-world test simulating concurrent 401s triggering a single token refresh, re-refresh after token expiry, and error propagation to all waiting requests.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Build (tsup)
pnpm build

# Run tests (bun test)
pnpm test
```

## Project Files

- [package.json](package.json): Project metadata, scripts, and npm configuration.
- [tsconfig.json](tsconfig.json): TypeScript compiler configuration.
- [tsup.config.ts](tsup.config.ts): Build configuration (ESM + CJS output).
- [LICENSE.txt](LICENSE.txt): MIT License.

- [Design spec](docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-singleflight-test-and-docs-design.md): Original design document for test and documentation structure.
